session_summary: Strategic design session exploring the limitations of the existing BIF pipeline (Pipeline A) and designing a new people-first Pipeline B that augments Module 2's demand-side intelligence using 30L Google reviews as revealed behavioral data.

topic_1_pipeline_a_diagnosis.what: Traced the full Pipeline A flow (step_3 → step_6) to understand what it actually does to review data
topic_1_pipeline_a_diagnosis.key_finding: Pipeline A is a venue intelligence tool. Every step moves further from the person and deeper into the venue. The human is extracted for signals in step_3 and then discarded. Output answers: 'What kind of place is this?' not 'Who are the people visiting this city?'
specific_losses_identified: Conditional logic flattened — 'food is good BUT service is pathetic' loses the tradeoff judgment; only individual signals survive
Person-level decision fingerprint discarded — why they came, what they tolerated, what caused exit — all lost after step_3 aggregation
Implicit behavioral identity signals not extracted — e.g. 'who goes dining and doesn't click pictures?' is a social identity signal, not a keyword match
Aggregation direction wrong for population intelligence — aggregates reviews→venue, not people→segments→population

topic_2_pipeline_b_concept.what: Designed a parallel Pipeline B that treats each review as one human behavioral record
topic_2_pipeline_b_concept.core_principle: Each review (deduped and algorithmically weighted) = one person. 30L reviews = behavioral data of 30L people. Pipeline B never aggregates upward to venue level.
person_fingerprint_fields: tradeoff_expressed (what compensated for what, or didn't)
social_behavior (brought friends / warned others / shared online)
topic_2_pipeline_b_concept.key_new_extraction_needed: Decision logic parsing — reading conjunctions and contrast markers (BUT, DESPITE, EVEN THOUGH) to understand tradeoff hierarchies. Everything else (negation, temporal, quality scoring) reusable from step_3.

topic_3_architecture_decision.status: DECIDED — separate pipeline, same raw input
topic_3_architecture_decision.what: Keeping Pipeline A untouched. Pipeline B runs on the same raw review JSON files but produces entirely separate outputs.
rationale: Different schemas from the start — Pipeline A produces venue records, Pipeline B produces person records
Different aggregation keys — Pipeline A keys on place_id, Pipeline B keys on behavioral segment IDs (cross-venue by design)
Different update cadence — Pipeline A updates per venue when new reviews arrive, Pipeline B updates when enough new people shift a segment
They reference each other without owning each other via cross-reference fields
new_db_tables_needed: venue_segment_density (which segments actually visit which venues)
archetype_validation (how Pipeline B segments map to the 35 survey archetypes)

topic_4_module_2_augmentation.what: Identified exactly how Pipeline B augments Module 2's gap analysis
topic_4_module_2_augmentation.current_gap_analysis_weakness: Demand side built from 45 survey responses (stated preferences). Supply side (venue primitives) is solid. Gap = weak demand side.
topic_4_module_2_augmentation.key_insight: The survey schema and Pipeline B measure the same variables through different methods. Survey = 45 people stating preferences. Pipeline B = 30L people revealing behavior. Pipeline B validates and enriches the demand side without replacing the survey.
topic_4_module_2_augmentation.gap_analysis_improvement_example: Current: Party Seeker wants live_music → venue lacks live_music → prescribe music. With Pipeline B: Party Seeker behavioral segment shows live_music absence in exit signals only 31% of the time; service dignity violations appear in 67% of exit signals. Intervention priority flips from music to service.

topic_5_data_source_audit.what: Audited all three pipeline instances to confirm primitive registry is shared

findings.primitives_json: Identical across all three pipeline locations (Google Places API, MagicPin BIF, Google Raw Scrapper BIF) — zero diff confirmed
findings.google_places_api: RUNNING — step_1 through step_6 complete, data in DB
findings.magicpin: RUNNING — step_1 through step_6 complete, data in DB
findings.google_raw_scrapper: STILL COLLECTING — reviews_worker_*.json accumulating, BIF scripts ready but not yet run on this data
findings.zomato: NOT STARTED — folder exists but empty
findings.source_weighting: Google 50%, MagicPin 20%, Zomato 30% (planned but Zomato not active yet)
findings.merge_point: Database layer, not pipeline layer — each source maintains separate primitive scores, combined score is weighted average

topic_5_data_source_audit.strategic_note: Google Raw Scrapper data is still pristine (unprocessed). Architecture decision for Pipeline B should be made before this data is committed to Pipeline A processing.

topic_6_module_3_preview.status: MENTIONED — to be discussed in future session
topic_6_module_3_preview.what: Brief mention that Pipeline B's person-level behavioral segments would sharpen Module 3 music psychology targeting
topic_6_module_3_preview.current_m3_limitation: Music prescriptions are archetype-level: 'Party Seekers → 120-130 BPM'. With Pipeline B: prescriptions could target specific behavioral sub-segments (which Party Seeker types respond to energy vs. intimacy vs. authenticity) and account for the actual friction causing exit before music even has a chance to work.

decisions_made: Pipeline B is a separate pipeline — not a replacement or modification of Pipeline A
Same raw review JSON files feed both pipelines
Pipeline B augments Module 2's demand side — survey archetypes get validated, not replaced
Google Raw Scrapper data architecture decision should be made before processing begins
next_steps: Design Stage 1 of Pipeline B — the person fingerprint extractor (decision logic parsing layer)
Decide whether Google Raw Scrapper data feeds Pipeline A, Pipeline B, or both
Discuss Module 3 music psychology targeting augmentation via Pipeline B segments
